notes: KNOWN FAILING — Gemini CLI v0.46.0 genuinely supports background processes (run_shell_command with is_background:true returns immediately with a background PID; the detached process keeps running). But the geminicli adapter (core/adapters/inbound/agents/geminicli/parser.go) has NO background-process tracking: it settles the session to ready as soon as the assistant message carries final text and opens no tool calls, so it does NOT hold the session working while a detached process is still alive. (The BackgroundProcessCount + HasLiveBackgroundProcess working-hold is wired only from the claudecode backgroundTaskId / tasks-output shape, which Gemini does not produce.) RECIPE: the prompt launches `sleep 40 && echo BG_DONE` with is_background:true and replies done IMMEDIATELY without polling — so the turn ends (~20s of tool round-trips) while the 40s bg process is still alive. CORRECT arc: ready→working, working HELD until the bg process exits (~40s), then a single working→ready. bg_alive_working asserts working persists for 28000ms (longer than the ~20s turn, shorter than the 40s bg lifetime) — the daemon settles to ready at turn-end (~20s) instead, so this phase is EXPECTED to fail ("state changed to ready after only ~20000 ms, expected to hold working for 28000 ms"). Fix is the Gemini parallel of #450/#445 in the geminicli parser; re-record and clear known_failing then.

text: Session appears in ready shortly after agent launch
User prompt drives the session ready → working

While the run_shell_command is_background process (sleep 40) is alive, the session must stay working — it must NOT settle to ready until the bg process exits (~40s). KNOWN FAILING: the geminicli adapter does not track background processes, so it settles to ready at the turn end (~20s) — well before the 28000 ms hold this phase requires.
After the background process exits (the list-background poll reports it gone and the liveness probe sees no live writer), the session settles working → ready
